evals.name: default-deny-dry-run-then-commit
evals.prompt: Lock down the payments namespace on prod-cluster with a default-deny.
evals.expected_output: Runs preflight, previews `cub-netpol default-deny payments --cluster prod-cluster` (dry-run, shows the YAML), and only on confirmation re-runs with --commit --change-desc. Notes the Unit is created but not applied. Does not kubectl.

evals.name: allow-from-links
evals.prompt: Generate the allow policies for apptique-prod from our Links.
evals.expected_output: Previews `cub-netpol allow-from-links --space apptique-prod` (one consolidated policy per destination from the dependency graph), then commits with --change-desc on confirmation. Mentions re-runs upsert/idempotent.

evals.name: fix-dns
evals.prompt: This egress default-deny is blocking DNS — add the kube-dns allowance to apptique-prod/default-deny-apptique.
evals.expected_output: Runs `cub-netpol fix dns apptique-prod/default-deny-apptique` (dry-run then --commit), which adds the kube-dns :53 egress allowance via set-yq; no-op if already present.

evals.name: routes-apply-elsewhere
evals.prompt: Now apply that default-deny to the cluster.
evals.expected_output: Does not apply from this skill; routes to cub-apply / `cub unit apply` for the rollout, noting netpol-fix only edits stored config.
